summary: Meridian now tells you what changed when it updates — plus Seraphism joins the mitigation planner's amplifier model.

sections.heading: Release notes in the app
items: A **What's new** popup appears the first time you launch a new version — including any releases you skipped along the way.
A **Version history** tab under Resources lists every release, newest first.

sections.heading: Mitigation planner
items: **Seraphism** is now modeled as a shield amplifier, alongside Zoe and Recitation — it scales every barrier cast inside its window instead of being counted as a standalone tool.

summary: Smarter mitigation planning, and a complete healer amplifier model.

sections.heading: Smarter mitigation planning
sections.body: The auto mit planner now allocates by scope and cost rather than bare survival:
items: Party-wide cooldowns are **banked off tank busters** — a raid mitigation spent on a single tank is no longer available for the raidwide that needs it.
Prevented damage is weighted by **who is actually hit**, so a party tool is no longer credited as if it covered eight players on a one-target hit.
Party mitigation and healer raid oGCDs now compete in **one value-ranked tier**, so healer raid cooldowns like Neutral Sect land on raidwides instead of being crowded out.
Spare oGCDs are spent to **cut the GCD-heal bill**, lowering the DPS tax the plan costs you.

sections.heading: Complete healer amplifier model
sections.body: Amplifiers now **scale the ability they power** instead of being counted as standalone tools, which makes double-counting impossible by construction:
items: **Shield riders** — Zoe and Recitation multiply the barrier they enable.

**Healing-boost windows** — Temperance, Philosophia, Krasis, Physis II, and Protraction scale healing inside their window (caster-scoped buffs boost the caster's own GCD spells; receiver-scoped buffs boost all incoming healing).

sections.body: Magnitudes were derived from the ability data and validated against top logs.

sections.heading: Note for healers
sections.body: Because the planner's healing tax is now more accurate, **healer efficiency percentages and ranks will shift** in this release. This is an intended recalibration, not a regression.

summary: More reliable auto-updates: fixes the "error opening file for writing" failures during install (the updater now keeps its background workers from re-locking files mid-update). Plus the healer mitigation planner's Sim Plan (BETA) is now bundled and on by default.

Healer mitigation planner: import hand-authored PF mit plans (e.g. Dancing Mad) and let the scheduler pick timing; healer progression analysis now reconciles the ceiling's healing tax against your actual uptime healing instead of penalizing every heal.

Fixes the auto-updater "error opening file for writing" errors: the sidecar now drains its sim-pool worker processes (which mapped the onedir _internal/ DLLs) before the NSIS install runs. Same analyzer feature set as 0.8.0.

sections.heading: Progression logs
items: Wipes are analyzable, not just kills — paste a report link or pick from your recent reports.
A pull that ends in a party wipe is **truncated at the death**, so the run isn't graded on time nobody was alive for.
Wipes report a **projected kill time** from the boss's remaining HP and your active damage rate.

sections.heading: Healer integration
items: Healers route through the **mitigation planner** first; the plan's heal GCDs are then locked into the idealized ceiling, so the ceiling pays the same healing tax you do.
That makes the ceiling an **honest maximum** rather than a pure-DPS fantasy — and rank comparisons are hidden for healers, since top logs force their DPS into different heal windows.

summary: First public release. Meridian scores your FFXIV raid pulls against a simulated optimal ceiling at your own GCD — with priced improvement suggestions, idealized-rotation timelines, comparisons against top logs (which get their own efficiency scores), and a kill-time theorizer that simulates an idealized potency rotation for a given kill time and party comp. Connects directly to your FF Logs account through their API.
